terms.definition: Unable to see. Helen Keller was blind - she could not see anything.
terms.exampleUsage: Helen Keller was blind from a very young age.

terms.definition: Unable to hear. Helen Keller was deaf - she could not hear any sounds.
terms.exampleUsage: Being deaf, Helen could not hear her teacher speak.

terms.definition: Having a strong will to achieve something, not giving up easily.
terms.exampleUsage: Helen was determined to learn to communicate.

terms.definition: The ability to face fear, danger, or difficulty bravely.
terms.exampleUsage: Helen showed great courage in overcoming her disabilities.

terms.definition: A word that describes a noun - tells us about its qualities like size, color, or nature.
terms.exampleUsage: In 'brave girl', the word 'brave' is an adjective.

terms.definition: To encourage someone or fill them with ideas to do great things.
terms.exampleUsage: Helen Keller's story inspires people all over the world.

terms.definition: To share information, ideas, or feelings with others.
terms.exampleUsage: Helen learned to communicate through sign language.

terms.definition: A way of communicating using hand movements and gestures instead of spoken words.
terms.exampleUsage: Anne Sullivan taught Helen sign language by spelling words into her palm.

terms.definition: The absence of light; when it is not bright.
terms.exampleUsage: The poem tells us not to be afraid of the dark.

terms.definition: Something difficult that you accomplish through effort and skill.
terms.exampleUsage: Graduating from college was a great achievement for Helen Keller.
